summary: Tested NotaryChain smart-contract async tailoring + public verify-by-hash + /my-anchors + /verify/contract flows. 8/9 backend tests PASS (one is intermittent due to BG-task worker blocking). Frontend: public verify, my-anchors list/detail, shareable verify-link, and notary anchor flow all work end-to-end. One CRITICAL backend bug found: the BackgroundTasks refactor does NOT actually achieve fire-and-forget — POST /render with ai_tailor=true still blocks ~17-23s and the very 502 risk it was meant to fix remains.

critical.issue: Refactor goal (instant response + background AI) is NOT achieved. The response is held ~17-23s on every call (warm and cold). Reproduced via curl: time_total=20.6s on first call, 19.4s on second call, both returning a tailor_job_id. Root cause: FastAPI/Starlette BackgroundTasks (background_tasks.add_task) execute AFTER the response is built but BEFORE uvicorn closes the request — the client and the ingress proxy still wait for the task to finish. This is exactly the cold-start 502 scenario the refactor was meant to prevent. Symptom-level evidence: subsequent /render calls intermittently 502 (`The preview environment is not responding`) when the previous BG task is still running and blocking the worker. Fix: replace `background_tasks.add_task(_run_tailor_job, ...)` with `asyncio.create_task(_run_tailor_job(...))` so the coroutine is truly detached from the request lifecycle, OR offload to a real task queue (Celery/RQ/arq). Today the polling endpoint /tailor-status works correctly, but the polling never gets a head start because /render itself blocks.

minor.issue: Intermittent 502 observed once immediately after a previous AI-tailor call — same root cause as above (worker held by background task). Passes 100% in isolation (0.42s).

ui_bugs.issue: The 'Tailoring with AI…' badge ([data-testid="tailoring-badge"]) was never observed during testing. Because the backend /render blocks until the BG-task finishes (~20s), the response already contains the AI-tailored content by the time the UI receives it, so the polling/badge code path never triggers. UI ends up showing the final draft and the 'AI tailoring complete' toast directly. Behaviorally OK, but the entire async-tailor UX (badge + polling) is dead code until the backend refactor is fixed.

action_items: FIX (CRITICAL): In /app/backend/routes/contract_template_routes.py replace `background_tasks.add_task(_run_tailor_job, job_id, rendered['content'], body.instructions, current_user.id)` with `asyncio.create_task(_run_tailor_job(job_id, rendered['content'], body.instructions, current_user.id))` and remove the `background_tasks: BackgroundTasks` parameter. Verify with `time curl ... ai_tailor=true` returns in <1s while /tailor-status/{job_id} eventually returns done.
Re-test the SmartContractTemplates 'Tailoring with AI…' badge after the fix — it should appear immediately after Regenerate and disappear when polling reports done.
critical_code_review_comments: BackgroundTasks misuse: FastAPI BackgroundTasks are scheduled BEFORE the worker is released — they do not give true fire-and-forget behavior across a buffering proxy. For long-running LLM calls, use `asyncio.create_task` (with a try/except wrapper to swallow exceptions) or a real queue.
verify_contract_by_hash correctly strips content/user_id from the public response — good. Continue to ensure no other fields leak (audit any future schema additions).

_run_tailor_job logs and stores `error` on failure, but the tailor_status endpoint does not surface the error message to the client (only status/content/ai_tailored). Consider exposing error so the UI can show 'fallback used' messaging.

success_rate.backend: 89% (8/9 — 1 intermittent due to BG-task worker block, root-caused above)
success_rate.frontend: 95% (5/6 verified flows; tailoring badge code path unverifiable due to backend block)

test_credentials: demo@test.com/Demo123!, notarytest@test.com/Test123! — both worked. Known anchored hash f76fb076c185a6df23a75a165d237a6f87b349e3af2ef8684968c06a353159ec verified as Verified & Untampered.
seed_data_creation: Test created one TEST_NDA Async-Verify anchor via notary account (via API + via UI), now visible in /my-anchors and verifiable at /verify/contract/<hash>.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The async tailoring backend route was supposed to be non-blocking but is NOT — BackgroundTasks still hold the worker. After main agent switches to asyncio.create_task (or a queue), re-run: (1) /app/backend/tests/test_contract_async_tailor_verify.py — the assertion `elapsed < 30` can be tightened to `elapsed < 2`; (2) UI test: Generate with ai_tailor=true and confirm [data-testid="tailoring-badge"] is visible, then polled to completion. Public verify, my-anchors, anchor detail modal, and notary anchor flow are all working — no re-test needed for those.
rca of the issue: RCA — slow /render with ai_tailor=true: Code at lines 144-146 of /app/backend/routes/contract_template_routes.py uses `background_tasks.add_task(_run_tailor_job, ...)`. Starlette's BackgroundTasks runs awaitable tasks AFTER constructing the Response object but BEFORE the underlying ASGI cycle returns control to uvicorn — the worker and the upstream proxy connection remain occupied until the GPT-5.2 call completes. Reproduction: `time curl -X POST .../render/nda -d '{...,"ai_tailor":true}'` → time_total ≈ 20s, body contains tailor_job_id (proof the BG task object was scheduled but waited). Mitigation: `import asyncio` and replace the add_task line with `asyncio.create_task(_run_tailor_job(job_id, rendered['content'], body.instructions, current_user.id))`. Wrap _run_tailor_job's body so its exceptions don't get swallowed silently (it already catches inside). The /tailor-status polling endpoint is correct and needs no change.
